sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 11 - Effect of an Early Election by an Insurance Company of an Operative Date under.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 11 - Effect of an Early Election by an Insurance Company of an Operative Date under

observedSectionWindows.note: Single-page interpretation note for the Section 5-C operative-date election language.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 11 PDF.
Do not widen into later guideline files.
Keep the operative-date / like-plan / generic-form language review-only until a human confirms the indexing decision.
Keep any PDF text-extraction noise visible as a caveat, not a promotion signal.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Effect of an early election of an operative date under Section 5-C of the Standard Nonforfeiture Law for Life Insurance
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep this slice within page 1 and do not absorb later guideline files.

proposedBatches.title: Single-page AG 11 interpretation

pageTarget.notes: Capture the whole one-page guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full one-page guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat explicit must, shall, or required language as the requirement; preserve the citation and source context.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat operative date, like plans, generic form, and cash values as terminology content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Treat the operative-date and plan-discontinuation references as context that needs source-bound preservation rather than promotion.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to Section 5-C, the Standard Nonforfeiture Law for Life Insurance, like plans, or generic form as mapping notes unless the operational text is included.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title line and framing sentence as background content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the one-page slice as a review-only window that should not be merged forward.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the indexing choice and the OCR / encoded text layer as requiring human actuarial interpretation unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.sourceQualityCaveat: Treat text-layer noise as review-only caveats and do not widen the batch because of them.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 11 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
The plan keeps the scope to one page only.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 11 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: Single-page guideline checks for future short guideline files.
Operative-date, like-plan, and generic-form tagging checks.
No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
Text-layer quality caveat checks once extraction starts.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag11-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-089 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 11 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch ID.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 11 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the VM-20 batches.

notes: Controlled AG 11 planning artifact. Planned batch 089 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
